problem.description: Live2D NPC character (Aura) fails to load in WebView. The HTML page loads from file:///android_asset/ but tries to fetch the Live2D model from an external CDN URL.
symptoms: [XHRLoader] Failed to load resource as json (Status 0): https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/...
Live2D Load Error: Error: Network error.
error_messages: Live2D Load Error: Error: Network error.
[XHRLoader] Failed to load resource as json (Status 0)

hypotheses.description: CDN URL fetched via XHR from file:// origin is blocked — Status 0 means no HTTP response received
Model assets not bundled locally — app depends on CDN availability at runtime

findings: Root cause: live2d.html hardcodes external CDN URL. XHR fails with status 0 (no internet on emulator or mixedContent blocked).
No model files bundled in assets — 100% CDN dependent.
Secondary: OnBackInvokedCallback not enabled in manifest.

resolution.root_cause: Live2D model fetched from https://cdn.jsdelivr.net CDN at runtime; XHR fails with Status 0 when device has no internet or WebView blocks mixed-content. No local model bundled.
resolution.fix_applied: 1) Added 8s load timeout + graceful animated CSS fallback in live2d.html (breathing anime silhouette with particles shown when CDN fails). 2) Added WebSettings.MIXED_CONTENT_ALWAYS_ALLOW + WebChromeClient to WebView config. 3) Added android:enableOnBackInvokedCallback=true to manifest. NOTE: permanent fix requires bundling Hiyori model files as local assets.
resolution.verification: When CDN is unreachable, app now shows animated Aura silhouette instead of blank screen. When CDN is reachable, Live2D loads normally.
